metadata.claimIdCollisionsObserved: Multiple sources reuse short claimIds (c1, c2, c3, a2_c1...) — non-globally-unique but disambiguated by sourceUrl; flagged as hygiene note, not a duplicate per spec.

unfollowedSignals: Visiting Student Program nav crumbs on src_76, src_89, src_93 — covered by the dedicated VISITING_STUDENT_PAGE (src_78), so nav-only crumbs are redundant rather than under-extracted.
Volunteer Clinical Professors nav reference — Tier 3 affiliated-faculty signal, captured as FUTURE_LANE only.
unresolveds: ClaimId collisions: short claim identifiers (c1, c2, c3, c4, c5, a2_c1, a2_c2, a2_c3, a2_c4) are reused across distinct sourceUrls. Disambiguated by sourceUrl, but downstream dedupe/merge should namespace claimIds by sourceId.

All 24 VISITING_STUDENT_PAGE (src_78) claims and the 2 explicit NEGATIVE refusal claims (c3/n1 international exclusion, n2 affiliated-only) were demoted by the reclassifier from PUBLIC_SAFE_USCE / PUBLIC_SAFE_NO_PUBLIC_OPPORTUNITY to HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED. A3 cannot upgrade, but human reviewer should confirm whether the affiliated-LCME-only refusal qualifies for PUBLIC_SAFE_NO_PUBLIC_OPPORTUNITY for the IMG audience.
verdictSummary: Zero PUBLIC_SAFE_USCE and zero PUBLIC_SAFE_NO_PUBLIC_OPPORTUNITY survived reclassification — all 80 merged claims sit in FUTURE_LANE_ONLY (17) or HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED (63). No scope conflicts (all sources on meded.ucsf.edu), no quote-vs-claim semantic gaps in the safe lanes, no over-promotion. Tier 1 (Visiting Student Program), Tier 2 (GME/ACGME/Match), and Tier 3 (Careers/Benefits/CME) all covered. Ledger is internally consistent and safe to merge; visiting-student and IMG-refusal claims correctly held back for human disposition.
